title: Delicious Dessert Scottish Eggs Eton Mess Pudding
description: A rich and creamy dessert pudding featuring caramel sauce and a twist on traditional Scottish eggs, all within the comfort of an Eton mess.

ingredients.name: Large egg yolks
ingredients.notes: Use room temperature egg yolks for easier emulsification.

ingredients.name: Heavy cream
ingredients.notes: Choose high-quality, full-fat heavy cream for the best results.

ingredients.name: Granulated sugar
ingredients.notes: Use a fine-grained sugar for even dissolving.

ingredients.name: Unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: Keep the butter at room temperature for easier melting.

ingredients.name: Caramel sauce
ingredients.notes: Make your own caramel sauce from scratch for a deeper flavor.

ingredients.name: Salted almonds
ingredients.notes: Choose a variety with a high salt content for added depth.

ingredients.name: Fresh raspberries
ingredients.notes: Use fresh, seasonal raspberries for the best flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: Confectioners' sugar
ingredients.notes: Sift the sugar before using to prevent lumps.

ingredients.name: Mantecare emulsifier
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality emulsifier for a smooth, stable pudding.

instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine the heavy cream, granulated sugar, and unsalted butter. Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar dissolves and the mixture reaches a simmer.
In a separate bowl, whisk the egg yolks until light and fluffy. Temper the egg yolks by slowly pouring the warm cream mixture into the egg yolks, whisking constantly.
Return the saucepan to the stovetop and heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until it coats the back of a spoon.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the mantecare emulsifier and salted almonds. Let the mixture cool slightly.
Fold in the fresh raspberries and confectioners' sugar until well combined.
Pour the pudding into individual serving cups or a large serving dish. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
Just before serving, drizzle the caramel sauce over the top of the pudding. Garnish with additional raspberries and almonds, if desired.
Serve chilled, with the caramel sauce drizzled on top.
